results.title: Focus with others through synchronized Pomodoro sessions
results.content:
Report

Yuki_Code1

Synchronized focus sessions add a nice social accountability layer that solo Pomodoro apps usually lack. How does matching work — do you get grouped with strangers working on similar goals, or is it more for pre-formed groups/friends?

Report

Ilya Shkurko

Grindoro

Grindoro

Maker

@ark\_y\_k Right now, there’s no goal-based matching or private friend groups. Everyone joins the same global session at the scheduled start time - usually on the hour or half-hour - alongside whoever else is focusing then.

I also don’t want users spending time deciding which room or group to join. Their main task is to focus, so the social layer should require almost no setup. [...] Hey Product Hunt 👋 I built Grindoro because I was tired of productivity apps that feel lonely. Most Pomodoro timers help you track time. Grindoro is designed to make focus feel like a shared ritual. Every session starts in a global rhythm — at the top of the hour or the 30-minute mark — so you’re not just starting a timer, you’re joining other people who are also trying to focus right now. Inside Grindoro, you can: – Start synchronized focus sessions – Use Light, Medium, or Dark Roast focus modes – Build routines, single tasks, and bigger goals – Manage your daily coffee energy – Earn XP and boosters – Collect weekly cafés as part of your focus passport The idea is simple: discipline feels easier when it feels alive. I’d love your feedback on the concept, the onboarding, and the weekly [...] Subscribe

results.title: ‎Grindoro: Pomodoro Focus Timer App - App Store
results.content:
Build better focus habits with a synchronized Pomodoro timer, smart tasks, routines, XP levels, and deep work sessions. Grindoro is a Pomodoro focus timer, habit tracker, and task planner built around synchronized deep work. Start focus sessions at the same time as people around the world, stay consistent with daily routines, and turn every completed session into visible progress. SYNCHRONIZED FOCUS Join global focus sessions that start at the top of the hour or 30-minute mark. Feel the momentum of working alongside others, even when you focus alone. POMODORO & DEEP WORK TIMER Choose the focus mode that matches your energy: Light Roast — 15-minute focus Medium Roast — 25-minute Pomodoro Dark Roast — 50-minute deep work HABIT TRACKER & ROUTINES Build daily consistency with routines. Track [...] ## Ratings & Reviews

 5.0

  out of 5

  11 Ratings

 ### New interesting approach

  saint\_sasha

  I have tried a lot of focus apps and most feel the same. This one feels different because of the community, when a session starts it feels like OK, everyone is working, I better get to work too!

  Developer Response

  Thanks! That shared feeling is exactly what we wanted to create. When more people focus together, the community can also activate stronger live momentum rewards.
 ### Great focus timer!

  ta\_voronova

  Feels more human than other productivity apps.I was looking for something between a habit app and a timer, this is perfect combination! i like routines and single tasks together. Some screens took me a minute to understand but overall really good!

  Developer Response [...] Developer Response

  Thanks for the thoughtful review! Along with Single tasks and Routines, Grindoro also supports long-term goals and progress-based tasks. We’re also working on making every screen easier to understand.
 ### My focusing improved a lot

  Kulinho7

  This is one of the few focus apps I didnt delete after two days. The coffee system is weird at first, but then it makes sense. it gives me a reason to be more intentional

  Developer Response

  Thanks for sticking with Grindoro! You can also collect a new café in your Coffee Passport by completing at least one focus session each week.
 ### Added to my daily used apps

  H.Papikyan

results.title: The Danger of the Pomodoro Method (why most people are ...
results.content:
enough time to shower so I can get changed and
get back to my Pomodoro." No, no, no, no. Just use the Pomodoro
method during break time. It's okay to take a one or two hour break to go to the gym or to have lunch. These are normal human things. Don't kill yourself here. But when you are in a Pomodoro session, you should stay focused. Trust me, if you are using
the Pomodoro method correctly, you will have more than enough
focus time during the day. (smooth jazz music) Tip number three is personalize the timing of your Pomodoro sessions. The timing doesn't have to be 25 minutes and a five minute break. I don't know why that's
become the cult of timing. Any times, it seems between
the 20 and 60 minute mark for your focus sessions are fine. You need to figure out what [...] in front of me that says, listen you have 10 minutes
go, don't break it. Cuz real thing is there. Big brother is watching. Importantly, also this removes another potential for distraction. If you have the timer on
your computer or your phone, you might more likely look at that and then get distracted or
pulled into something else, even if the only thing you
do is make studying nicer, it's a small price to pay
for making, studying nicer especially when you're studying maybe five to 10 hours a day. (smooth jazz music) The next tip is remove
all possible distractions. Eliminate the possibility of being distracted during
your Pomodoro sessions. Please remove all unnecessary
items from your desk, all books that you're not using, all notes that you're not gonna be using, your phone especially, [...] computer, it's study time. When you're in your Pomodoro sessions, you're signaled unconsciously,
if you do it enough, that this is study time. Again, this is the most vital
tip of this entire video. If it is study time, if you
are in a Pomodoro session, you must stay focused. Oh, and this applies to your breaks too. When it's break time, go take a break. Don't study during your break time, cuz that would mess things up. Again, if you're gonna study
through your break time make it another Pomodoro session. That way you're extending your flow and not messing with
classical conditioning. (smooth jazz music) Okay, three more tips here. And the third to last tip is music. Use it correctly for you. Music is a tough topic around studying. There's no strong answers. There's no strong decision

results.title: The Problem With The Pomodoro Technique 🕔
results.content: when i had these assignments that were really big and i had to really put in my thinking cap and get to the nitty-gritty of problem solving this is because when i wanted to use my brain for heavy lifting and i was using the pomodoro method at the same time i found that it would often interrupt my workflow when i was doing these 30 minutes to an hour intervals for example if i'm ideating and brainstorming for my next creative project i'm on a roll and my brain is in this complete state of focus however in the moment that the pomodoro timer sets off i've conditioned myself to kind of let go of that concentration and take the time to relax and sit back i'll just be scrolling on my phone maybe doing another task in between and sometimes i'll go back to the project at hand or i just won't come [...] i have an issue with the pomodoro timer let me tell you why the pomodoro technique consists of working in typically 25 minute intervals and allowing yourself to have quick breaks in between a lot of people find it effective because it allows for you to stay hyper focused and free of distractions when i first heard about this method in high school i thought it was super effective and it allowed for me to power through huge chunks of when i would be studying for tests or exams but it was only effective up to a certain point although it was effective for periods when i was doing menial tasks that i didn't really want to do or studying for exams i found that it wasn't necessarily the best for when i wanted to do like creative work or when i had these assignments that were really big and i had [...] be interrupted when you're in a flow state and you're not pushing yourself to work through these 25 minute intervals when you don't really feel like working in anticipation of the break from the timer let us know if there are any focus methods that you're currently trying out and how it's been helping you we'd love to hear from you guys until next time bye

results.title: Grindr is rated "Bad" with 1.1 / 5 on Trustpilot
results.content:
Jul 3, 2026

Image 34: Rated 1 out of 5 stars

## Frustrating experience with poor filtering and no control
The app has become very frustrating to use. It used to feel more focused, but now everything is mixed together with very little control over who you see.

There are many profiles from people who don’t seem interested in actually meeting men, which doesn’t make sense for what is supposed to be a GAY APP. It makes finding genuine matches much harder.

The biggest issue is filtering — it simply doesn’t work properly. You are constantly shown profiles that don’t match your preferences, and many users don’t include basic information in their profiles, which makes things even worse. There should be a requirement to include essential details.
